source_paper_title: Proximity and Flatness Bounds for Linear Integer Optimization
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Celaya_2024, title={Proximity and Flatness Bounds for Linear Integer Optimization}, volume={49}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0335}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0335}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Celaya, Marcel and Kuhlmann, Stefan and Paat, Joseph and Weismantel, Robert}, year={2024}, month=nov, pages={2446–2467} }
context:
Page 3, Introduction (discussion following Theorem 4)

It is open whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded solely by \(\Delta_n(A)\).
title: Bound lattice width of lattice-free polytopes using only determinant minor parameter
background_latex:
Let $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$ have full column rank and let $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$. Define the (rational) polyhedron
\[
P(A,b):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: Ax\le b\}.
\]
The polyhedron is called lattice-free if $P(A,b)\cap\mathbb{Z}^n=\emptyset$.

For a nonzero direction $a\in\mathbb{R}^n\setminus\{0\}$, define the width of $P(A,b)$ in direction $a$ by
\[
w_a(P(A,b)):=\max_{x\in P(A,b)} a^\top x-\min_{y\in P(A,b)} a^\top y.
\]
The lattice width is
\[
w(P(A,b)):=\min_{a\in\mathbb{Z}^n\setminus\{0\}} w_a(P(A,b)).
\]

For $k\in\{1,\dots,n\}$ define the maximal absolute $k\times k$ minor of $A$ by
\[
\Delta_k(A):=\max\{ |\det M| : M \text{ is a } k\times k \text{ submatrix of } A\}.
\]
In particular, $\Delta_n(A)$ is the maximum absolute determinant of an $n\times n$ submatrix of $A$ (which is nonzero because $A$ has full column rank $n$).
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether there exists a function $f:\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}$ such that for every dimension $n\ge 2$, every full-column-rank matrix $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$, and every $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$ for which $P(A,b)$ is a (full-dimensional) lattice-free polyhedron, the lattice width satisfies
\[
w(P(A,b))\le f(\Delta_n(A)).
\]
Equivalently, decide whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded above by a function depending only on $\Delta_n(A)$ (and not on $n$).
latex:
Let $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$ have full column rank and let $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$. Define the (rational) polyhedron
\[
P(A,b):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: Ax\le b\}.
\]
The polyhedron is called lattice-free if $P(A,b)\cap\mathbb{Z}^n=\emptyset$.

For a nonzero direction $a\in\mathbb{R}^n\setminus\{0\}$, define the width of $P(A,b)$ in direction $a$ by
\[
w_a(P(A,b)):=\max_{x\in P(A,b)} a^\top x-\min_{y\in P(A,b)} a^\top y.
\]
The lattice width is
\[
w(P(A,b)):=\min_{a\in\mathbb{Z}^n\setminus\{0\}} w_a(P(A,b)).
\]

For $k\in\{1,\dots,n\}$ define the maximal absolute $k\times k$ minor of $A$ by
\[
\Delta_k(A):=\max\{ |\det M| : M \text{ is a } k\times k \text{ submatrix of } A\}.
\]
In particular, $\Delta_n(A)$ is the maximum absolute determinant of an $n\times n$ submatrix of $A$ (which is nonzero because $A$ has full column rank $n$).

Determine whether there exists a function $f:\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}$ such that for every dimension $n\ge 2$, every full-column-rank matrix $A\in\mathbb{Z}^{m\times n}$, and every $b\in\mathbb{Z}^m$ for which $P(A,b)$ is a (full-dimensional) lattice-free polyhedron, the lattice width satisfies
\[
w(P(A,b))\le f(\Delta_n(A)).
\]
Equivalently, decide whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded above by a function depending only on $\Delta_n(A)$ (and not on $n$).
open_problem_quote: It is open whether the lattice width of lattice-free polytopes can be bounded solely by \(\Delta_n(A)\).
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can computationally search for families of lattice-free polytopes with fixed $\Delta_n(A)$ and large lattice width by enumerating small integer matrices and right-hand sides and checking lattice-freeness and width via MILP/LP. However, the search space grows quickly with $n,m$ and certifying worst-case growth may require guided constructions beyond brute force.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate counterexamples can be represented programmatically as integer matrices $A$ and integer vectors $b$, with fitness given by computed lattice width subject to a constraint on $\Delta_n(A)$ and lattice-freeness. The challenge is efficiently enforcing full-dimensional lattice-freeness and exploring high-dimensional instances, but evolutionary search could plausibly discover structured families with large width or suggest sharper conjectured bounds.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
